name: Apple Retail Experience
summary: Consumer-facing features and internal retail tools for Apple.com — built for accessibility, performance, and scale across hundreds of millions of users.
problem: Apple's retail experience required seamless, accessible product discovery and purchase flows that performed flawlessly across devices — at a scale of hundreds of millions of users.
whatIDid: Contributed to frontend development of key Apple.com retail features, with a focus on accessibility (WCAG AA+), performance optimization, and cross-browser consistency. Collaborated closely with design and QA teams under high-stakes launch conditions.
outcome: Features shipped globally to 100M+ users. Zero critical accessibility regressions on launch.

name: Nike Running Campaign
summary: A scroll-driven campaign landing page translating a broadcast concept into a digital-first experience with strong brand continuity.
problem: The campaign needed to carry the energy of a high-production TV spot into a web experience — without losing momentum or brand integrity in the translation.
whatIDid: Led frontend development of an animated landing page with scroll-driven storytelling, performance-optimized video, and a live leaderboard component. Worked directly with the Nike brand team to stay true to the visual language.
outcome: 2.1M unique visitors in the first week. 68% average scroll depth — well above industry benchmarks.

name: LaunchKit
summary: A productized launch system for solo founders — combining strategy, design, development, and launch tools to help ideas go live faster.
problem: Solo founders kept reinventing the same infrastructure — landing pages, waitlists, email sequences — from scratch every time. The cost in time and energy was killing momentum before products even launched.
whatIDid: Designed and built a set of reusable templates, components, and step-by-step workflow guides bundled as a self-serve digital product. Covered everything from pre-launch to first 100 users.
outcome: 500+ founders in the first cohort. $18K in first-month revenue. Now a growing community product.

name: Meridian Studio
summary: Mobile-first website and integrated booking experience for a boutique creative business, designed for visibility, conversion, and day-to-day usability.
problem: The studio was losing clients to a dated, non-mobile site with no online booking capability. Inquiries were falling through the cracks and the brand wasn't being represented at the level the work deserved.
whatIDid: Designed and built a fully responsive site with integrated scheduling, a custom CMS for content updates, and performance-optimized image delivery. Handled everything from discovery through launch.
outcome: 40% increase in online inquiry conversion within 60 days of launch. Client now manages content independently.

name: Aloha Animal Outreach
summary: Website redesign and digital strategy for an Oahu-based animal rescue, supporting outreach, trust-building, and community engagement across the island.
problem: AAO's aging site was actively limiting donations, volunteer signups, and pet adoptions. The organization was doing incredible work that no one could easily find or act on.
whatIDid: Rebuilt the site from scratch with an optimized donation flow, event listings, and an adoptable pet gallery. Also designed and ran a fundraising email campaign, creating all copy and visuals.
outcome: Donations increased 120% in year one. Volunteer applications tripled. The pet gallery became the highest-traffic page on the site.
